examTip: This is the highest-frequency Arithmetic topic. Appears in almost every SI exam. Master the shortcuts below — they save 30-40 seconds per question.

sections.heading: ⚡ Core Formulas — Memorize These
points: If X increases by A% then decreases by A% → Net change = −(A²/100)% — ALWAYS a loss

sections.heading: 🧠 The Biggest Exam Trap — Same SP Equal P&L
points: When 2 articles sold at SAME SP — one at X% profit, one at X% loss → ALWAYS a LOSS overall
Example: SP = ₹990 each, 10% profit + 10% loss → Loss% = 10²/100 = 1%
This trap appears in EVERY WB SI exam. Never say 'no profit no loss'.

sections.heading: 📊 Successive Discount Shortcut
points: Two discounts A% and B% → Equivalent single discount = A + B − (AB/100)
Three discounts: apply two at a time using same formula

sections.heading: 💡 Speed Tricks for Profit-Loss
points: CP of X articles = SP of Y articles → Profit% = (X−Y)/Y × 100, Loss% = (Y−X)/X × 100
Marked Price: if profit after discount, MP = SP / (1 − discount%) and SP = CP × (1 + profit%)

items.question: A shopkeeper marks goods 25% above CP and gives 10% discount. Find profit%.
Two articles sold at ₹560 each, one at 12% profit and one at 12% loss. Net?
items.solution: Same SP with equal P&L → Loss% = 12²/100 = 1.44%. Always a loss.

items.question: If 40% of a number is 160, what is 75% of the number?
